cases.focus: published post query, default date ordering, LIMIT, and ID result materialization
post__in filtering and FIELD() order preservation
search SQL over title/content/excerpt and result ordering
WP_Tax_Query join/where generation against term relationship tables

live_boundaries_covered: real wpdb mysqli connection and query execution
WP_Query SQL request construction for posts, search, meta, and taxonomy cases
WP_Meta_Query postmeta join/where generation against live rows
WP_Tax_Query term relationship join/where generation against live rows

native_boundaries.reason: This fixture isolates WP_Query SQL/result behavior. Generic WordPress services around hooks, cache, options, users, post type registration, and taxonomy registration are deterministic stubs while the query classes and wpdb execution remain upstream PHP.

WPHX-307.08 expands the live WP_Query slice across the locked MySQL 8.4, MariaDB 11.8, PHP 8.4 DB-client, and PHP 8.5 DB-client matrix. Upstream PHPUnit and installed-distribution ratchets remain follow-up closure for the posts/query workset.

run_summaries.command: docker run --rm --network <network> -v <repo>:/work -w /work wordpresshx/php-db-client:8.4-cli-mysqli-pdo-mysql php build/wp-core/wphx-307-07/probe.php oracle build/wp-core/wphx-307-07/oracle db 3306 root <password> wordpresshx_wpquery_oracle mysql-8.4:php-8.4-db-client

cases.request: SELECT wp_posts.ID FROM wp_posts WHERE 1=1 AND ((wp_posts.post_type = 'post' AND (wp_posts.post_status = 'publish'))) ORDER BY wp_posts.post_date DESC LIMIT 0, 3

SELECT wp_posts.ID FROM wp_posts WHERE 1=1 AND wp_posts.ID IN (101,102,105) AND ((wp_posts.post_type = 'post' AND (wp_posts.post_status = 'publish'))) ORDER BY FIELD(wp_posts.ID,105,101,102) LIMIT 0, 5

SELECT wp_posts.ID FROM wp_posts WHERE 1=1 AND (((wp_posts.post_title LIKE '%Alpha%') OR (wp_posts.post_excerpt LIKE '%Alpha%') OR (wp_posts.post_content LIKE '%Alpha%'))) AND (wp_posts.post_password = '') AND ((wp_posts.post_type = 'post' AND (wp_posts.post_status = 'publish'))) ORDER BY wp_posts.post_date DESC LIMIT 0, 5

SELECT wp_posts.ID FROM wp_posts INNER JOIN wp_postmeta ON ( wp_posts.ID = wp_postmeta.post_id ) WHERE 1=1 AND ( ( wp_postmeta.meta_key = 'wphx_color' AND wp_postmeta.meta_value = 'blue' ) ) AND ((wp_posts.post_type = 'post' AND (wp_posts.post_status = 'publish'))) GROUP BY wp_posts.ID ORDER BY wp_posts.ID ASC LIMIT 0, 5

SELECT wp_posts.ID FROM wp_posts LEFT JOIN wp_term_relationships ON (wp_posts.ID = wp_term_relationships.object_id) WHERE 1=1 AND ( wp_term_relationships.term_taxonomy_id IN (70) ) AND ((wp_posts.post_type = 'post' AND (wp_posts.post_status = 'publish'))) GROUP BY wp_posts.ID ORDER BY wp_posts.ID ASC LIMIT 0, 5
SELECT wp_posts.ID FROM wp_posts WHERE 1=1 AND ((wp_posts.post_type = 'post' AND (wp_posts.post_status = 'publish'))) ORDER BY wp_posts.post_date DESC LIMIT 0, 3

remaining_gaps.detail: The candidate side remains a copied WordPress oracle source tree until WP_Query and related post/query behavior move behind Haxe-owned public PHP output.
The fixture stubs surrounding WordPress services to isolate query SQL/result parity. Installed WordPress and upstream PHPUnit query suites remain required closure gates.
The live WP_Query slice now covers the pinned MySQL/MariaDB and PHP DB-client matrix, but executable upstream PHPUnit ratchets and installed WordPress distribution behavior remain follow-up gates.
